name: Lavash
recipeInstructions:
1. Lightly oil a bowl for the dough.
2. Mix the yeast, warm water, and sugar in a large mixing bowl.
3. Add the flour and salt to the mixture and stir until it forms a well-blended but somewhat soft dough (avoid adding too much more flour).
4. Knead the dough by hand: Turn it out onto a floured board and work it until smooth and elastic, about 10 minutes. Alternatively, knead on an electric mixer's lowest speed for about 5 minutes.
5. Pat the dough into a ball and place in the oiled bowl. Cover with a kitchen towel and let rise until doubled in bulk (30 to 40 minutes). A gas oven's pilot light heat or an electric oven set low for no more than 2 minutes before turning off works well.
6. When risen, turn out onto a floured board, punch down, and knead again until air is expelled.
7. Divide into 8 round mounds, place on the board, cover with a towel, and let rise until almost doubled (about 30 minutes).
8. Preheat oven to 450°F and position rack close to the bottom.
9. Flour a 12x15-inch baking sheet.
10. When risen, roll each mound into rectangles about 12x15 inches and as thin as for pizza.
11. Puncture surface at ½-inch intervals with a roasting fork.
12. Bake in batches for 6 to 8 minutes or until lightly browned on top.
13. Remove from oven to wire rack to cool; repeat baking remaining breads until all are done.
14. If large bubbles rise, puncture immediately with a fork during baking.
15. Traditionally, the Middle Eastern flat leavened bread is served as a cracker. To serve with meals, moisten each cracker under cold running water on both sides and place in a plastic bag for 3 hours. The bread will become pliable and chewy.
(End of recipe)
